description:
In SocketManager.cleanup_dead_connections(), there is a race condition that can cause double-decrement of IP counts:

1. cleanup_dead_connections() iterates @connections while holding @mutex
2. It removes each dead connection from @connections (delete_at)  
3. It calls unregister_connection(conn) OUTSIDE the mutex

But unregister() also modifies @connections:
1. unregister() finds connection, marks as unregistered, removes from @connections
2. Calls decrement_ip_count_locked(ip) inside mutex

Race scenario:
1. cleanup_dead_connections() iterates and collects conn with unregistered:false
2. Before it calls unregister_connection(), unregister() runs
3. unregister() marks conn as unregistered and removes it from @connections
4. cleanup_dead_connections() calls unregister_connection() on the collected conn
5. unregister_connection sees unregistered:false (copied before unregister ran)
6. Both unregister() AND unregister_connection() decrement IP count

The unregistered flag protection only works if the flag state is checked AFTER acquiring the mutex. Currently the flag check happens outside the mutex in unregister_connection(), creating the race.

Code location: src/websocket/socket_manager.cr - cleanup_dead_connections and unregister methods
title: Bug: SocketManager double-decrement in cleanup_dead_connections race
